title: Joining Through a Bridge Table
summary: Connect two entity tables through the enrollment records that relate them.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding Bridge Tables
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
A bridge table is used to connect two tables that have a many-to-many relationship. In our school database, the `enrollments` table acts as a bridge between `students` and `courses`. Each enrollment record links a student to a course, allowing us to query information about which students are enrolled in which courses.

For example, consider the following SQL query:

```sql
SELECT students.name, courses.title
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id;
```

This query retrieves the names of students along with the titles of the courses they are enrolled in. The `enrollments` table is crucial here as it provides the necessary link between `students` and `courses`. By joining through the `enrollments` table, we can access data from both related tables.

sketchBlocks.title: Building Complex Queries with Bridge Tables
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
When working with bridge tables, it's important to understand how to extend queries to include additional related data. For instance, if we want to include department information for each course a student is enrolled in, we can extend our query by joining the `departments` table.

Here's how you can do it:

```sql
SELECT students.name, courses.title, departments.name AS department
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
```

In this query, we have added another join to include the `departments` table. This allows us to retrieve not only the student names and course titles but also the department names associated with each course. By understanding the relationships and using bridge tables effectively, you can build complex queries that provide comprehensive insights into your data.

quizDraft.title: Querying Student Enrollments
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all students and the courses they are enrolled in, using the `enrollments` table as a bridge.
quizDraft.hint: Join the `students` and `courses` tables through the `enrollments` table.

help.concept: Use the `enrollments` table to connect `students` and `courses`.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT students.name, courses.title
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT students.name, courses.title
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id;

tests.stdout:
Mia | Biology
Mia | Algebra II
Leo | Algebra II
Leo | World History
Ava | Computer Basics
Noah | Biology
Noah | Computer Basics
Emma | World History

quizDraft.title: Extending Queries with Departments
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all students, the courses they are enrolled in, and the corresponding department for each course.
quizDraft.hint: Extend the previous query to include the `departments` table.

help.concept: Join the `departments` table to include department names.
help.hint_2: Select department names using an alias for clarity.

quizDraft.starterCode:
SELECT students.name, courses.title, departments.name AS department
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;
quizDraft.solutionCode:
SELECT students.name, courses.title, departments.name AS department
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id
JOIN departments ON courses.department_id = departments.id;

tests.stdout:
Mia | Biology | Science
Mia | Algebra II | Math
Leo | Algebra II | Math
Leo | World History | Humanities
Ava | Computer Basics | Technology
Noah | Biology | Science
Noah | Computer Basics | Technology
Emma | World History | Humanities

quizDraft.title: Purpose of a Bridge Table
quizDraft.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a bridge table in a database?
quizDraft.hint: Think about how bridge tables connect other tables.

help.concept: Bridge tables connect two tables that have a many-to-many relationship.
help.hint_1: Bridge tables help in linking two tables indirectly.
help.hint_2: They are used to manage many-to-many relationships.

options: To connect two tables with a many-to-many relationship

quizDraft.title: Identifying Bridge Tables
quizDraft.prompt: Which table acts as a bridge table between `students` and `courses` in the school database?
quizDraft.hint: Look for the table that connects `students` and `courses`.

help.hint_1: The bridge table contains foreign keys to both `students` and `courses`.
help.hint_2: It records which students are enrolled in which courses.

quizDraft.title: SQL Join Syntax
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL statement to join `students` and `enrollments` on `student_id`.
quizDraft.hint: Use the correct SQL syntax for joining tables.

help.concept: The `ON` clause specifies the condition for joining tables.

quizDraft.template: SELECT * FROM students JOIN enrollments ON [blank1];

quizDraft.title: Extending a Query
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL statement to join `courses` and `departments` on `department_id`.
quizDraft.hint: Use the correct SQL syntax for joining tables.

help.concept: The `ON` clause specifies the condition for joining tables.

quizDraft.template: SELECT * FROM courses JOIN departments ON [blank1];

quizDraft.title: Characteristics of Bridge Tables
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are characteristics of a bridge table? Select all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Consider the role of bridge tables in managing relationships.

help.concept: Bridge tables manage many-to-many relationships by linking two tables.
help.hint_1: They contain foreign keys to both related tables.
help.hint_2: They do not store redundant data but facilitate connections.

options: Contains foreign keys to related tables

quizDraft.title: Order of SQL Join Operations
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the steps to join `students`, `enrollments`, and `courses` in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Start with the table that contains the primary key.

help.concept: The order of joins should follow the relationship path from primary to foreign keys.

tokens: JOIN students to enrollments on student_id
JOIN enrollments to courses on course_id
SELECT columns from students, enrollments, and courses
correctOrder: SELECT columns from students, enrollments, and courses
JOIN students to enrollments on student_id
JOIN enrollments to courses on course_id
